name: Claude platform MCP production patterns
current_usage_summary: Anthropic positions MCP as the compounding layer for production agents that run in the cloud and must reach remote SaaS with standardized auth and portability. Claude Cowork, Managed Agents, and Claude Code channels consume remote MCP servers. Integrators ship intent-grouped tools (not 1:1 API mirrors), optional MCP Apps for inline UI, and elicitation for mid-flight user input. Plugins bundle MCP servers with skills. Directory has 200+ servers; SDK downloads cited at 300M/month (Apr 2026 blog).
typical_stack: Remote HTTPS MCP servers (Streamable HTTP preferred over legacy SSE). Client: Claude.ai, Claude Code, Managed Agents runtime. Auth: OAuth 2.1 + CIMD for frictionless client registration; Managed Agents Vaults store user OAuth tokens, injected per session with refresh. Skills (.md) + MCP tools composed in plugins. Large-surface vendors use Code Mode pattern (search + execute in sandbox).
auth_and_identity_model: Resource owner authorizes via OAuth; access tokens presented to MCP server per request. Vault abstraction for cloud agents: register tokens once, reference vault ID at session creation—platform handles refresh. Contrasts with CLI ambient credentials on disk. Multi-tenant SaaS expects per-user OAuth, not shared API keys. MCP spec mandates Protected Resource Metadata for auth server discovery on HTTP transports.
context_and_token_behavior: Platform pushes context efficiency: Tool Search defers tool definitions (~85% reduction, Anthropic testing); Programmatic Tool Calling runs loops in sandbox (~37% token reduction on complex workflows). Code Mode reduces Cloudflare-scale APIs from ~1M+ tool tokens to ~1K. These are client exposure policies—protocol unchanged. Skills add procedural knowledge without duplicating schemas.
operational_pain_points: Building excellent remote servers is upfront cost. Vault + OAuth compliance burden for ISVs. Large API surfaces still risk context blowups without Code Mode or tool search. Extension ecosystem (skills-from-server) still experimental.
developer_experience_friction: Must learn MCP tool design guides (intent grouping, effective tools blog). Dual maintenance: public API + MCP + sometimes CLI. Certification/directory requirements for distribution.
gax_mapping: GAX does not replace Claude client—it sits as execution/runtime alternative integrators can target. Mapping: MCP server tools → GAX command manifests; Vault → GAX tenant vault + cap mint; Tool Search/Code Mode → native `gax search`/`gax doc` + `surface=model` projection + in-gaxd jq/plan. For Managed Agents, gaxd could register as a single MCP server exposing only meta-tools (search, invoke, doc) mirroring Code Mode—one schema in context.
migration_phases: 1) ISV publishes GAX manifests alongside MCP (same backend). 2) Offer 'GAX mode' MCP server that proxies to gaxd for customers wanting caps/audit. 3) Enterprise runs private gaxd; Claude Managed Agent connects via thin MCP shim. 4) Long-term: ACSP spec recognized by hosts alongside MCP (spec politics uncertain).
adapter_or_bridge_strategy: Thin MCP façade: tools `gax_invoke`, `gax_search`, `gax_doc` delegating to gaxd—same strategy as Cloudflare Code Mode. Existing rich MCP servers get wrapped by manifest per tool mapping. Skills updated to reference `gax gh.pr.list` strings instead of tool names.
breaking_changes_and_mitigations: MCP Apps and elicitation not in GAX v0.1—mitigate by keeping native MCP for UI-heavy flows during transition. Vault IDs vs GAX caps—mitigate with vault→cap exchange at session start in hosted gaxd.
infrastructure_changes: ISV: deploy gaxd next to existing MCP server or embed gaxd library in-process. Enterprise: hosted gaxd with SSO + OTel; policy bundles synced from Git. Claude-side may still need one MCP connection unless Anthropic adds native GAX/ACSP.
deployment_topology: SaaS integrator: multi-tenant gaxd with per-tenant policy; public MCP endpoint unchanged for backward compat. Enterprise customer: private gaxd in VPC; outbound only to SaaS APIs; audit stream to SIEM.
adoption_drivers: Enterprises already mandated OAuth for MCP—GAX adds per-invoke caps and uniform audit_id without abandoning OAuth investment. ISVs reduce tool count exposed to any client via lazy GAX discovery.
adoption_blockers: Anthropic explicitly advocates MCP as critical cloud layer—GAX is complementary challenger, not endorsed standard. Integrators follow directory traffic—MCP marketplace gravity. Managed Agents optimized for MCP vaults.
adoption_timeline_estimate: GAX-as-thin-MCP-shim viable in 12–18 months for security-conscious enterprises; native ACSP in major clients 3+ years without consortium backing.
historical_parallel: gRPC alongside REST: new transport for governance-heavy paths while REST stays for browsers—GAX alongside MCP for governed agent calls.

primary_sources.title: Building agents that reach production systems with MCP
Anthropic advanced tool use / tool search
